steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank contains the preposition “of,” which connects the noun “achievements” to its object—the noun phrase “artists of Latin American descent.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for prepositions
steps.content: A preposition attaches directly to its object without intervening punctuation. The answer is D because “of” must connect seamlessly to “artists of Latin American descent.” Choices A, B, and C all insert punctuation (a semicolon, colon, or comma) between the preposition and its object. That matches choice D — D is correct.
